prompt: The first and most important step in decontamination is:

options.text: Cleaning to remove soil/bioburden before disinfection or sterilization
Sterilizing heavily soiled instruments without cleaning
Skipping enzymatic detergents always when soil is present
Using sterile storage wraps as cleaning cloths

explanation: Soil shields microbes from sterilants; cleaning is prerequisite to sterilization.

prompt: PPE in decontamination typically includes:

options.text: Street clothes only
Fluid-resistant gown, gloves, mask/eye protection, and appropriate footwear controls
Sterile surgical gloves as the only PPE with bare arms
No eye protection when splashing

explanation: Decontam is the dirtiest area—dress for splash/sharps hazards.

prompt: Manual cleaning scrubbing should be done:

options.text: With vigorous spraying upward toward the face
Using abrasive pads that scratch all surfaces indiscriminately against IFU
Below the water line to reduce aerosols, using appropriate brushes
Without following device IFU

explanation: Minimize aerosols; select brushes that contact lumens/crevices without damaging devices.

prompt: Lumened devices require:

options.text: Only wiping the outside
No attention to inner channels
Sterilization without cleaning if lumen is short
Brushing and flushing according to IFU, including correct brush diameter

explanation: Biofilm in lumens is a major failure point (e.g., endoscopes/complex instruments).

prompt: Enzymatic detergents are useful because they:

options.text: Break down organic soils (protein, fat, carbs) when used at correct temperature/contact time
Sterilize instruments alone
Replace rinsing
Work better if mixed to random strengths

explanation: Follow concentration, temp, and time; rinse thoroughly to remove residues.

prompt: Gross soil should be removed:

options.text: Allowed to dry for days to 'set' soil
As soon as possible at point of use, keeping instruments moist per protocol
By tossing instruments into sharps containers
Using sterile saline bags as hammers

explanation: Point-of-use pretreatment prevents hardened soil and corrosion.

distractorExplanations.c: Instruments ≠ sharps disposal route for reusable sets.

prompt: A negative air pressure relationship is typically maintained in:

options.text: The sterile storage as more negative than decontam
Office break rooms vs OR always as the SPD rule
The decontamination area relative to clean areas
The parking lot

explanation: Air flows into decontam so contaminants don't blow into clean prep/sterile areas.

distractorExplanations.b: Break rooms not the SPD air design focus.

prompt: When receiving loaner instruments, SPD should:

options.text: Send straight to OR without processing because they look shiny
Skip BI monitoring forever for loaners
Store in decontam uncovered indefinitely
Inspect, clean, and sterilize according to IFU before use—never assume they are ready

explanation: Loaners are high risk for inadequate processing history.

prompt: Ultrasonic cavitation cleaning requires:

options.text: Degassing, proper dilution, basket loading without overstacking, and rinsing after
Overloading trays tightly with no solution contact
Mixing dissimilar metals carelessly causing damage always as best practice
No manufacturer instructions

explanation: Follow ultrasonic IFU; some devices cannot be ultrasonicated.

prompt: Chemical disinfection high-level (HLD) is used for:

options.text: Critical implants as the preferred permanent substitute for sterilization always
Semicritical items that contact mucous membranes when sterilization isn't feasible per Spaulding/IFU
Environmental floors as sterilant
Skin antisepsis as surgical sterilant

explanation: Critical items require sterilization; HLD is not a sterilant unless a sterilant cycle/claim applies.

prompt: The first step in instrument reprocessing after point-of-use preparation is typically:

options.text: Immediate sterilization without cleaning
Thorough cleaning/decontamination to remove soil before disinfection or sterilization
Skipping detergent if instruments look clean
Lubricating hinged instruments before any rinse

explanation: Soil must be removed; sterilization cannot compensate for inadequate cleaning.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Skipping detergent if instruments look clean
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Lubricating hinged instruments before any rinse

prompt: Personal protective equipment in decontamination should include:

options.text: Street clothes only
Fluid-resistant attire, eye protection, and gloves appropriate to splash/sharps risk
No eye protection if sinks have faucets
Open-toed shoes for comfort

explanation: Decontam PPE protects against bloodborne pathogens and chemical splash.

distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: No eye protection if sinks have faucets

prompt: Enzymatic detergents are used in cleaning primarily to:

options.text: Sterilize instruments in 30 seconds
Break down organic soil such as blood and protein
Replace biological indicators
Raise sterilizer chamber temperature

explanation: Enzymes help digest organic soils to improve cleaning efficacy.

prompt: Why should lumened instruments be flushed according to IFU during cleaning?

options.text: Flushing is never necessary for any lumen
Bioburden and debris can remain inside channels if not properly irrigated
Flushing replaces the need for sterilization
It only matters for wooden instruments

explanation: Lumens trap soil; IFU-directed flushing/brushing is critical.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Flushing is never necessary for any lumen
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Flushing replaces the need for sterilization
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: It only matters for wooden instruments

prompt: Gross soil should be removed at point of use mainly to:

options.text: Decorate the OR back table
Prevent drying of soil that makes later cleaning more difficult and less effective
Activate the sterilizer cycle early
Eliminate the need for PPE in SPD

explanation: Point-of-use removal/keeping instruments moist improves subsequent decontamination.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Decorate the OR back table
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Activate the sterilizer cycle early
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Eliminate the need for PPE in SPD

prompt: Instruments should be inspected for:

options.text: Cleanliness, function, alignment, corrosion, and completeness before packaging
Only the count sheet color
Nothing if they came from washers
Rust as acceptable for sterile use

explanation: Assembly is a quality checkpoint; repair/replace defective instruments.

distractorExplanations.b: Count sheets help but inspection needed.

prompt: Rigid container systems require:

options.text: Cracked lids accepted if taped heavily
Intact filters/valves/gaskets and correct locking per IFU, with internal CI as required
No filter checks
Overfilling above rated weight always

explanation: Containers fail if seals/filters compromised; follow load weight limits.

prompt: Woven/nonwoven wraps should be:

options.text: Used when soaking wet
Closed with safety pins through the wrap freely always
Sized appropriately using sequential wrapping techniques without tenting/strangulation of pack
Reusable forever without inspection

explanation: Wrap integrity and technique maintain sterility barrier.

prompt: Chemical indicators are placed:

options.text: Only on the sterilizer wall outside
Never inside sets
In the OR after opening only as the sole placement
In areas of each pack/tray challenging to sterilant penetration per policy/IFU

explanation: Internal CI helps detect sterilant penetration problems inside packs.

prompt: Count sheets in trays help:

options.text: Ensure complete sets and document contents for OR accountability
Replace cleaning
Sterilize devices
Serve as biological indicators

explanation: Incomplete trays delay surgery and risk counts.

prompt: Instruments with lumens may need:

options.text: Grease packing inside lumens before sterilizing
Moisture/residual water controls and lumen accessories per sterilizer IFU (e.g., wetness management)
Tape sealing lumen ends closed always
No special attention

explanation: Steam must contact surfaces; excess water can cause wet packs; some systems use lumen devices.

prompt: Tip protectors are used to:

options.text: Block steam intentionally as their purpose
Replace wrap
Protect sharp/delicate tips and may aid sterilant contact if designed permeable
Indicate BI results

explanation: Use protectors validated for sterilization compatibility.

prompt: Tray weight limits exist because overweight trays can cause:

options.text: Faster cycles always with better kill
Automatic BI passes
Sharper scissors
Wet packs, poor drying, and sterilization failures

explanation: Follow sterilizer and container IFU for maximum weights.

prompt: Assembly area air quality should be:

options.text: Cleaner/positive relative to decontamination, controlled for lint/dust
More contaminated than decontam
Open to outside dust winds ideally
Identical to soiled utility without controls

explanation: Protect clean instruments during assembly/packaging.

prompt: Missing instruments identified at assembly should be:

options.text: Ignored if only one scissor missing in a major set
Documented and resolved before sterilization/OR delivery per policy
Replaced with random unprocessed instruments from decontam floor
Hidden from OR

explanation: Incomplete sets are patient safety issues.

prompt: Instruments should be inspected for cleanliness and function:

options.text: Only after the patient leaves the hospital
During assembly, before packaging for sterilization
Never, if the washer completed a cycle
Only when a surgeon complains

explanation: Visual/functional inspection at assembly catches residual soil and defects.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Only after the patient leaves the hospital
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Never, if the washer completed a cycle
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Only when a surgeon complains

prompt: Which statement about rigid sterilization containers is correct?

options.text: Filters/valves and gaskets must be intact and used per IFU
Damaged filters are acceptable if tape is applied
Containers never need cleaning between uses
Locking mechanisms are decorative only

explanation: Container integrity (filters, seals, locks) is required for sterility maintenance.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Damaged filters are acceptable if tape is applied
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Containers never need cleaning between uses
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Locking mechanisms are decorative only

prompt: Hinged instruments should generally be sterilized:

options.text: Fully closed and locked tightly every time
In the open or unlocked position to allow sterilant contact
Wrapped while soaking wet with blood
Without stringers or racks even if IFU requires them

explanation: Open position allows steam/sterilant to contact box locks and jaws.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Fully closed and locked tightly every time
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Wrapped while soaking wet with blood
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Without stringers or racks even if IFU requires them

prompt: Chemical indicators (internal) placed in packs primarily show:

options.text: That sterility is guaranteed for 10 years
That sterilant conditions were met at the indicator location (process conditions), not sterility by themselves
The exact CFU count of remaining organisms
That the washer-disinfector was skipped

explanation: CIs monitor critical process parameters; BIs are needed for lethality verification.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: That sterility is guaranteed for 10 years
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: The exact CFU count of remaining organisms
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: That the washer-disinfector was skipped

prompt: Count sheets in trays are used to:

options.text: Replace biological monitoring
Verify completeness of instrument sets during assembly and at point of use
Document employee lunch breaks
Increase sterilizer temperature

explanation: Count sheets support accurate assembly and OR counts.

prompt: Steam sterilization kills microorganisms primarily by:

options.text: Coagulation/denaturation of proteins via moist heat under pressure
Freezing cells
Only UV light inside every autoclave load
Desiccation alone at room temp

explanation: Saturated steam transfers heat efficiently; air removal critical in prevac systems.

prompt: Prevacuum sterilizers use a Bowie-Dick type test to detect:

options.text: Exact BI spore species only
Air removal effectiveness / air leaks in dynamic-air-removal systems
Detergent concentration
Package aesthetics

explanation: Bowie-Dick is a daily diagnostic for prevac air removal—not a BI.

prompt: Geobacillus stearothermophilus spores are used to monitor:

options.text: Ethylene oxide exclusively always
Dry heat only always
Steam sterilization biological indicators
Radiation only

explanation: Match BI organism to sterilant: stearothermophilus for steam; subtilis/niger historically for EO/dry heat variants—know current IFU.

distractorExplanations.a: EO uses different spore historically/currently per product.

prompt: A wet pack after steam sterilization should be considered:

options.text: Sterile if cool to touch
OK if used within 5 minutes always
Fixed by wiping with a towel and issuing
Contaminated / not sterile for use; reprocess after investigating cause

explanation: Moisture wicks microbes through wraps; find loading/drying/weight causes.

prompt: Ethylene oxide (EO) sterilization is chosen when:

options.text: Devices are heat/moisture sensitive and IFU specifies EO, with aeration afterward
Immediate use without aeration is always safe
It is faster than steam for all stainless sets routinely
No monitoring is needed

explanation: EO is toxic/flammable concerns historically; aeration removes residuals; BI monitoring required.

prompt: Hydrogen peroxide vapor/plasma systems are often used for:

options.text: Linen packs soaked with liquids always
Heat-sensitive devices compatible with the sterilizer IFU
Powders and oils as ideal loads
Any device with long dead-end lumens against IFU

explanation: Compatibility lists matter; cellulose/liquids often contraindicated in some systems.

prompt: Load control/sterility assurance documentation should include:

options.text: Only the operator's favorite music
No records to save paper always
Cycle parameters, CI/BI results, load contents, and operator identification per policy
Patient SSN on every sterilizer strip publicly

explanation: Traceability supports recalls and accreditation.

prompt: Implant loads should be:

options.text: Released early without BI always as best practice
Never monitored
Washed only
Quarantined until BI results are known (CDC recommendation concept), when possible

explanation: Implants are high risk—await BI before release when feasible.

prompt: Superheating in steam sterilization can occur when:

options.text: Steam is too dry / dehydrated, reducing sterilizing efficiency
Packages are correctly moist with saturated steam conditions
BI passes prove superheating always desirable
Air is perfectly removed and steam saturated ideally as the definition of superheat

explanation: Saturated steam is required; superheated steam behaves more like dry heat at that temperature.

distractorExplanations.c: BI pass doesn't define superheat as good.

prompt: Gravity displacement sterilizers remove air by:

options.text: High-speed vacuum pulses always like prevac
Steam entering and forcing cooler air out through a drain passively
Ultrasonic cavitation
EO gas

explanation: Gravity cycles need proper loading to avoid air trapping; longer times often than prevac.

prompt: Steam sterilization efficacy depends heavily on:

options.text: Color of the sterilizer paint
Time, temperature, and saturated steam contact (and proper drying/air removal as designed)
Leaving packages soaking wet with detergent
Skipping Bowie-Dick tests on dynamic-air-removal sterilizers forever

explanation: Steam sterilization requires adequate sterilant contact under validated parameters.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Color of the sterilizer paint
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Leaving packages soaking wet with detergent
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Skipping Bowie-Dick tests on dynamic-air-removal sterilizers forever

prompt: A biological indicator (BI) is used to:

options.text: Decorate the sterilizer door
Challenge the sterilization process with resistant spores to verify lethality
Measure water hardness only
Replace cleaning verification

explanation: BIs provide the highest level of sterilization process challenge.

prompt: IUSS (immediate-use steam sterilization) should be:

options.text: Used as the routine method for all loaner sets
Limited to urgent situations with documented justification and proper cleaning first
Performed on packaged implants without monitoring
Done without following IFU

explanation: IUSS is for immediate need after proper cleaning—not a substitute for routine processing.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Used as the routine method for all loaner sets
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Performed on packaged implants without monitoring

prompt: Ethylene oxide (EO) sterilization is often chosen for:

options.text: Items that are heat- and moisture-sensitive when validated for EO
All stainless bowls exclusively
Only cardboard shipping boxes
Items that were never cleaned

explanation: EO can sterilize many heat/moisture-sensitive devices when compatible and aerated properly.

distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Items that were never cleaned

prompt: A wet pack after steam sterilization is a concern because:

options.text: Moisture can create a pathway for contamination and indicates a process/packaging problem
Wet packs are always more sterile
Moisture proves the BI passed
Wetness means the load can skip cooling

explanation: Wet packs compromise sterility assurance and require investigation per policy.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Wet packs are always more sterile
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Moisture proves the BI passed
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Wetness means the load can skip cooling

prompt: Sterile storage should be:

options.text: Clean, dry, well-ventilated, above the floor, away from sinks/windows moisture
Under a leaking sink
On the floor against traffic dirt
In decontam next to soak sinks

explanation: Maintain package integrity; positive pressure clean areas preferred.

prompt: Stock rotation uses:

options.text: LIFO always as the only sterile storage rule
FIFO (first in, first out) so older sterile packs are used first
Random grab without dates/lot awareness
Keeping expired event-compromised packs 'just in case'

explanation: FIFO reduces outdates and aging damage.

prompt: Before issuing a sterile pack to OR, SPD/user should check:

options.text: Only whether the tape is pretty
Nothing if the cart arrived
Integrity of packaging, dryness, indicator response, and expiration/event status
Whether the surgeon likes the color

explanation: At-point inspection is the last safety net.

prompt: Transportation of sterile items requires:

options.text: Open arms stacking packs against scrub tops casually through rain
Placing packs on the floor of the elevator
Using decontam case carts without cleaning between soiled/clean roles
Covered/closed carts and clean handling to protect packages

explanation: Separate clean/soiled logistics; clean carts between uses.

distractorExplanations.c: Case cart roles must not cross-contaminate.

prompt: IUSS items transported to OR must be:

options.text: Aseptically transferred and used immediately—not stored
Put on shelves for later cases that week
Cooled uncovered in a hallway traffic stream
Placed under sinks

explanation: Immediate-use items aren't inventory stock.

prompt: Quality assurance in distribution includes:

options.text: Avoiding all documentation
Tracking loaners, implants, and sterilization lot information for traceability
Discarding BI logs monthly without retention policy
Handwriting patient secrets on tape publicly

explanation: Traceability enables recalls if BI failures/product issues arise.

prompt: Shelf-life of sterile packs depends heavily on:

options.text: The color of the wrap dye alone
How loudly the sterilizer beeped
Storage conditions and package integrity (event-related) or facility dating policy
OR temperature preferences only

explanation: Even with dates, inspect for compromise events.

prompt: If a sterile package is dropped on the floor:

options.text: Wipe with sleeve and use
Use if dropped 'only once'
Spray with room air freshener
Consider it contaminated and reprocess (unless a validated rigid container IFU says otherwise for specific cases)

explanation: Floor contact contaminates wraps; rigid containers have specific IFU exceptions sometimes.

prompt: Inventory levels should balance:

options.text: Enough stock for procedures without overstocking that leads to damage/outdates
Zero extras always causing constant IUSS
Infinite piles blocking sprinklers/air vents
Storing sterile with soiled

explanation: PAR levels and case cart systems improve OR support.

prompt: Recalls of sterilized loads are initiated when:

options.text: A staff member changes haircut
BI failure or other sterilizer malfunction potentially affects released items
The coffee pot breaks
All packages look intact with perfect records and passing BI

explanation: Have a written recall procedure with risk assessment and OR notification.

distractorExplanations.d: No recall if truly no failure indication.

prompt: Sterile storage areas should be:

options.text: Damp, dirty, and near decontamination splash zones
Clean, dry, well-ventilated, and protected from traffic contamination
Open to outdoor weather
Used for eating lunch

explanation: Environmental controls protect package integrity until use.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Damp, dirty, and near decontamination splash zones

prompt: Event-related sterility means sterility is maintained until:

options.text: A fixed calendar date only, regardless of package condition
An event compromises the package (e.g., moisture, tears, improper handling), subject to facility policy
The manufacturer recalls all indicators
The item leaves the sterilizer cart for 5 minutes

explanation: Many facilities use event-related dating; integrity events outdate the item.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: A fixed calendar date only, regardless of package condition
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: The manufacturer recalls all indicators
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: The item leaves the sterilizer cart for 5 minutes

prompt: When transporting sterile packages to the OR, staff should:

options.text: Carry uncovered trays against scrub uniforms through rain
Use clean covered carts/containers and handle packages to protect integrity
Stack heavy items on delicate peel packs without support
Open packs in the elevator to cool them

explanation: Covered transport and careful handling prevent contamination and damage.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Carry uncovered trays against scrub uniforms through rain
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Stack heavy items on delicate peel packs without support
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Open packs in the elevator to cool them

prompt: First in, first out (FIFO) rotation of sterile stock helps:

options.text: Ensure older stock is used before newer stock within dating/policy rules
Hide expired implants at the back forever
Increase wet pack rates
Eliminate the need for inventory counts

explanation: FIFO reduces outdates and supports inventory control.

distractorExplanations.b: Incorrect: Hide expired implants at the back forever
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Eliminate the need for inventory counts

prompt: If a sterile package falls on the floor, best practice is generally to:

options.text: Wipe it with a sleeve and return it to sterile storage
Consider it contaminated and reprocess according to facility policy
Use it only for the next emergency IUSS without cleaning
Relabel it with a new sterilization load sticker only

explanation: Dropped packages are treated as contaminated unless policy allows a validated exception.

distractorExplanations.a: Incorrect: Wipe it with a sleeve and return it to sterile storage
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect: Use it only for the next emergency IUSS without cleaning
distractorExplanations.d: Incorrect: Relabel it with a new sterilization load sticker only
